exercises.title: Provider Abstraction Layer
exercises.description: Build a comprehensive provider abstraction layer that works with IBM, Braket, and Azure Quantum.
tasks: Define abstract QuantumProvider class with submit, status, result, and cancel methods
Implement IBMQuantumProvider with Qiskit-compatible interface
Implement BraketProvider with AWS Braket-compatible interface
Implement AzureQuantumProvider with Azure-compatible interface
Create ProviderFactory that instantiates providers from configuration
exercises.expectedOutput: Working provider abstraction with factory pattern
hints: IBM uses job.status() returning strings like 'QUEUED'
Normalize status strings to JobStatus enum
Factory should handle missing credentials gracefully

exercises.title: Multi-Cloud Orchestrator
exercises.description: Create an orchestrator that manages job submission and result collection across multiple providers.
tasks: Register and manage multiple providers
Submit same circuit to multiple backends in parallel
Collect results with timeout handling
Implement provider health checking
Support automatic load balancing across providers
exercises.expectedOutput: Full orchestrator with parallel submission and health checking
hints: Health check: try listing backends with timeout
Store job_id -> provider mapping for result collection
Rebalancing: cancel and resubmit on healthy provider

exercises.title: Cross-Provider Result Analyzer
exercises.description: Build analysis tools to compare and validate results across different quantum providers.
tasks: Calculate TVD (Total Variation Distance) between result distributions
Compute classical fidelity between distributions
Find consensus distribution from multiple results
Identify statistical outliers
Generate comparison report
exercises.expectedOutput: Analyzer with TVD, fidelity, consensus, and outlier detection
hints: Normalize counts to probabilities before comparison
Consensus: weighted average by shot count

exercises.title: Resilient Execution Framework
exercises.description: Implement resilience patterns for reliable multi-cloud quantum execution.
tasks: Implement circuit breaker pattern with open/closed/half-open states
Add hedged execution (parallel submit, first wins)
Implement retry with exponential backoff per provider
Create fallback chain (try providers in priority order)
Add timeout handling at job and batch levels
exercises.starterCode:
from enum import Enum
import time
import threading
from typing import List, Tuple, Optional, Callable
from concurrent.futures import ThreadPoolExecutor, as_completed

class BreakerState(Enum):
    CLOSED = 'closed'     # Normal operation
    OPEN = 'open'         # Failing, reject requests
    HALF_OPEN = 'half_open'  # Testing recovery

@dataclass
class CircuitBreaker:
    state: BreakerState
    failure_count: int
    last_failure_time: float
    success_count_in_half_open: int

class ResilientExecutor:
    def __init__(self, orchestrator: MultiCloudOrchestrator):
        self.orchestrator = orchestrator
        self.breakers: Dict[str, CircuitBreaker] = {}
        self.failure_threshold = 3
        self.recovery_timeout = 300
        self.half_open_success_threshold = 2
    
    def get_breaker(self, provider: str, backend: str) -> CircuitBreaker:
        """Get or create circuit breaker for backend."""
        pass
    
    def can_execute(self, provider: str, backend: str) -> bool:
        """Check if circuit breaker allows execution."""
        pass
    
    def record_success(self, provider: str, backend: str) -> None:
        """Record successful execution."""
        pass
    
    def record_failure(self, provider: str, backend: str) -> None:
        """Record failed execution."""
        pass
    
    def execute_with_breaker(self, circuit: UnifiedCircuit,
                            provider: str, backend: str,
                            shots: int) -> UnifiedResult:
        """Execute with circuit breaker protection."""
        pass
    
    def execute_hedged(self, circuit: UnifiedCircuit,
                      targets: List[Tuple[str, str]],
                      shots: int,
                      return_first: bool = True) -> List[UnifiedResult]:
        """Execute on multiple backends, optionally return first."""
        pass
    
    def execute_with_fallback(self, circuit: UnifiedCircuit,
                             provider_chain: List[Tuple[str, str]],
                             shots: int) -> UnifiedResult:
        """Try providers in order until one succeeds."""
        pass
    
    def execute_with_retry(self, circuit: UnifiedCircuit,
                          provider: str, backend: str,
                          shots: int,
                          max_retries: int = 3,
                          base_delay: float = 1.0) -> UnifiedResult:
        """Execute with exponential backoff retry."""
        pass
exercises.expectedOutput: Resilient executor with circuit breaker, hedging, and retry
hints: Hedging: use as_completed to get first success
